unknown: 'Sibling tool call errored' cascade: When parallel Bash calls are made and one is blocked by Sentinel, Claude Code marks all sibling calls as errored. Is this Claude Code core behavior or something we can control? Need to verify if this is configurable or fundamental to Claude Code's parallel execution model.
resolved_by: This is Claude Code core behavior, not Empirica. When Claude sends parallel tool calls and ONE is blocked by a PreToolUse hook, Claude Code aborts ALL sibling calls with 'Sibling tool call errored'. This is a safety feature to prevent partial/inconsistent state. The fix is: (1) ensure Sentinel whitelist covers all read-only commands so they don't get blocked, (2) avoid parallel Bash calls when some might be gated. Root cause here was missing whitelist entries which is now fixed.
